questions.question: Which is the IPv6 “private” (RFC1918-like) range commonly used?
questions.explanation: Unique Local is FC00::/7, used in practice as FD00::/8.

questions.question: Which multicast address reaches all routers on a link?
questions.explanation: FF02::2 = all routers; FF02::1 = all nodes.

correct.explanation: FF02::2 = all routers; FF02::1 = all nodes.

incorrect.explanation: **FF02::1** describes a different mechanism than this question tests. The right idea: FF02::2 = all routers; FF02::1 = all nodes.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Picking a familiar term without matching the exact behavior tested

incorrect.explanation: **FF02::5** describes a different mechanism than this question tests. The right idea: FF02::2 = all routers; FF02::1 = all nodes.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Picking a familiar term without matching the exact behavior tested

incorrect.explanation: **224.0.0.2** describes a different mechanism than this question tests. The right idea: FF02::2 = all routers; FF02::1 = all nodes.

questions.question: True or False: IPv6 uses a broadcast address like IPv4.
questions.explanation: False — IPv6 has no broadcast; multicast (FF00::/8) replaces it.

correct.explanation: False — IPv6 has no broadcast; multicast (FF00::/8) replaces it.

incorrect.explanation: This statement is **True**, but the tested fact is: False — IPv6 has no broadcast; multicast (FF00::/8) replaces it.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Flipping the true/false fact without matching the scenario details

questions.question: The same IPv6 address is configured on several servers and traffic goes to the nearest. What type is this?
questions.explanation: Anycast = one address on multiple devices, delivered to the nearest.

questions.question: Which multicast group does every IPv6 host join?
questions.explanation: All IPv6 hosts join FF02::1, the all-nodes group.

correct.explanation: All IPv6 hosts join FF02::1, the all-nodes group.

incorrect.explanation: **FF02::2 (all routers)** describes a different mechanism than this question tests. The right idea: All IPv6 hosts join FF02::1, the all-nodes group.
